sections.title: Section 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
Directions: In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Listen to an example. 

On the recording, you hear: 
(man) That exam was just awful. 
(woman) Oh, it could have been worse. 
(narrator) What does the woman mean? 

In your test book, you read: 
(A) The exam was really awful. 
(B) It was the worst exam she had ever seen. 
(C) It couldn't have been more difficult. 
(D) It wasn't that hard. 

You learn from the conversation that the man thought the exam was very difficult and that the woman disagreed with the man. The best answer to the question, "What does the woman mean?" is (D), "It wasn't that hard." Therefore, the correct choice is (D).

questions.passage:
M: Carla said that you were rather rude.
W: It's unfair of her to say that about me.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Carla does not live very far away.

reason.en: This option is a distractor based on the similar-sounding word 'far' which is not present in the dialogue, and it is irrelevant to the context of the conversation.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah pengecoh yang didasarkan pada kata yang terdengar mirip 'far' yang tidak ada dalam dialog, dan tidak relevan dengan konteks percakapan.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The woman states that Carla's comment is 'unfair', which is a direct synonym for 'unjust'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Wanita tersebut menyatakan bahwa komentar Carla 'unfair' (tidak adil), yang merupakan sinonim langsung dari 'unjust' (tidak adil/tidak pantas).

options.text: He does not fear what anyone says.

reason.en: This option is incorrect as it misinterprets the meaning of the dialogue and introduces the concept of 'fear' which is not mentioned.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena menyalahartikan makna dialog dan memperkenalkan konsep 'takut' yang tidak disebutkan.

options.text: Carla is fairly rude to others.

reason.en: This option is a distractor that uses the word 'rude' from the dialogue to create a false interpretation of the situation.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah pengecoh yang menggunakan kata 'rude' dari dialog untuk menciptakan interpretasi situasi yang salah.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
M: I don't think this painting is very good.
W: It's better than the first one, isn't it?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT THE PAINTING?

reason.en: The woman states the painting is 'better than the first one,' which is a synonym for an improvement.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan lukisan itu 'lebih baik dari yang pertama,' yang merupakan sinonim dari sebuah peningkatan (improvement).

options.text: The fir trees in it are better.

reason.en: This is a 'similar sound' trap; 'first one' in the dialogue sounds like 'fir trees,' which is irrelevant to the context.
reason.id: Ini adalah jebakan 'bunyi serupa'; 'first one' dalam dialog terdengar seperti 'fir trees,' yang tidak relevan dengan konteks.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states it is better than the first one, not that it resembles it.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan lukisan itu lebih baik dari yang pertama, bukan menyerupainya.

options.text: It is the best the man has ever done.

reason.en: This is a misinterpretation; the woman only compares the current painting to the first one, not to all of the man's work.
reason.id: Ini adalah salah tafsir; wanita tersebut hanya membandingkan lukisan saat ini dengan yang pertama, bukan dengan semua karya pria tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Your graduation ceremony is this afternoon.
M: I can't believe it. I've graduated at last!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He graduated last in his class.

reason.en: This option is a trap based on a surface-level interpretation of the word 'last', which is used idiomatically in the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah jebakan berdasarkan interpretasi permukaan dari kata 'last', yang digunakan secara idiomatik dalam dialog.

options.text: He is the last person in his family to graduate.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the phrase 'at last' as referring to the man's position within his family, which is not mentioned.
reason.id: Opsi ini secara keliru menginterpretasikan frasa 'at last' sebagai posisi pria tersebut dalam keluarganya, yang tidak disebutkan.

options.text: He doesn't believe he can improve gradually.

reason.en: This option uses words that sound similar to 'graduated' (gradually) to create a distractor, which is a common trap in Skill 3.
reason.id: Opsi ini menggunakan kata-kata yang terdengar mirip dengan 'graduated' (gradually) untuk membuat pengecoh, yang merupakan jebakan umum dalam Skill 3.

options.text: He has finally finished his studies.

reason.en: The phrase 'at last' is a synonym for 'finally', and 'graduated' indicates the completion of studies, matching the man's statement.
reason.id: Frasa 'at last' adalah sinonim dari 'finally', dan 'graduated' menunjukkan penyelesaian studi, yang sesuai dengan pernyataan pria tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: I got this dress for only five dollars!
M: Five dollars! How did you get it so cheap?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He's surprised there were five dresses.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the number 'five' as a quantity of dresses rather than the price.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menginterpretasikan angka 'lima' sebagai jumlah gaun, bukan sebagai harga.

options.text: It was an unexpectedly inexpensive dress.

reason.en: The man's repetition of 'Five dollars!' and his question 'How did you get it so cheap?' clearly indicate his surprise at the low price of the dress.
reason.id: Pengulangan 'Five dollars!' oleh pria tersebut dan pertanyaannya 'How did you get it so cheap?' dengan jelas menunjukkan keterkejutannya atas harga gaun yang murah.

options.text: He would like to know what color dress it was.

reason.en: The conversation focuses on the price of the dress, not its color; this is an irrelevant detail.
reason.id: Percakapan berfokus pada harga gaun, bukan warnanya; ini adalah detail yang tidak relevan.

reason.en: This contradicts the man's own statement, as he explicitly asks how the woman managed to get it 'so cheap'.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu sendiri, karena ia secara eksplisit bertanya bagaimana wanita itu bisa mendapatkannya dengan harga 'begitu murah'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 13: Listen for Emphatic Expressions of Surprise

questions.passage:
W: I just got my third parking ticket this week.
M: Why don't you try putting more money in the parking meter when you park your car?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SUGGEST THAT THE MAN DO?

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the man suggests putting more money in the meter, not moving the car to a different location.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena pria tersebut menyarankan untuk memasukkan lebih banyak uang ke meteran, bukan memindahkan mobil ke lokasi lain.

reason.en: This option is incorrect as it contradicts the man's advice to avoid getting more tickets by paying the meter.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena bertentangan dengan saran pria tersebut untuk menghindari denda lebih lanjut dengan membayar meteran.

options.text: Add more money to the meter.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. The man explicitly suggests putting more money in the parking meter, which is paraphrased as adding more money to the meter.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyarankan untuk memasukkan lebih banyak uang ke meteran parkir, yang diparafrasekan sebagai menambahkan lebih banyak uang ke meteran.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the dialogue mentions the parking meter, not a parking attendant.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena dialog menyebutkan meteran parkir, bukan petugas parkir.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
W: Were you able to get hold of the book that you wanted?
M: I couldn't. At the bookstore, they told me that it wasn't available yet.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He does not like to hold too many books at one time.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the phrase 'get hold of' as a literal physical action of holding books, which is incorrect in this context.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah mengartikan frasa 'get hold of' sebagai tindakan fisik memegang buku, yang tidak tepat dalam konteks ini.

options.text: There is no bookstore in his neighborhood.

reason.en: The dialogue mentions that the man went to a bookstore, so the claim that there is no bookstore in his neighborhood is not supported.
reason.id: Percakapan menyebutkan bahwa pria tersebut pergi ke toko buku, jadi klaim bahwa tidak ada toko buku di lingkungannya tidak didukung.

options.text: It's not possible to obtain the book yet.

reason.en: This is the correct answer. It paraphrases the man's statement 'it wasn't available yet' using the synonym 'not possible to obtain the book yet'.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar. Opsi ini memparafrase pernyataan pria tersebut 'it wasn't available yet' menggunakan sinonim 'not possible to obtain the book yet'.

options.text: He needs to talk to someone at the bookstore.

reason.en: The man has already spoken to someone at the bookstore, so this does not reflect the current status of his request.
reason.id: Pria tersebut sudah berbicara dengan seseorang di toko buku, jadi ini tidak mencerminkan status permintaan dia saat ini.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: Professor Mitchell's lecture certainly went on and on for quite some time.
M: I thought he was never going to finish.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY ABOUT THE LECTURE?

reason.en: The dialogue does not suggest the lecture was incomplete; rather, it implies it was excessively long.
reason.id: Dialog tidak menyarankan bahwa kuliah tersebut tidak lengkap; sebaliknya, dialog menyiratkan bahwa kuliah tersebut terlalu panjang.

reason.en: The man's comment 'I thought he was never going to finish' contradicts the idea that it finished on time.
reason.id: Komentar pria tersebut 'Saya pikir dia tidak akan pernah selesai' bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa kuliah selesai tepat waktu.

reason.en: This is a distractor based on the similar sound of 'honor' and 'on and on'.
reason.id: Ini adalah pengecoh yang didasarkan pada kemiripan bunyi antara 'honor' dan 'on and on'.

reason.en: The man agrees with the woman's observation that the lecture went on for a long time, implying it was too long.
reason.id: Pria tersebut setuju dengan pengamatan wanita bahwa kuliah berlangsung lama, yang menyiratkan bahwa kuliah tersebut terlalu panjang.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: You don't have the notes from yester day's physics class? 
W: No, I don't. Do you think I could borrow yours?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She needs to use the man's notes.

reason.en: The woman explicitly asks, 'Do you think I could borrow yours?' which directly paraphrases the need to use the man's notes.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut secara eksplisit bertanya, 'Apakah menurutmu aku bisa meminjam milikmu?' yang secara langsung memparafrasekan kebutuhan untuk menggunakan catatan pria tersebut.

options.text: Yesterday's physics class was quite boring.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention the quality of the class, making this an irrelevant interpretation.
reason.id: Percakapan tidak menyebutkan kualitas kelas tersebut, sehingga ini adalah interpretasi yang tidak relevan.

options.text: She took some very good notes in physics class.

reason.en: This contradicts the woman's statement that she does not have the notes from yesterday's class.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan wanita tersebut bahwa dia tidak memiliki catatan dari kelas kemarin.

options.text: She would like to lend the man her  notes.

reason.en: This is the opposite of the woman's request; she is the one asking to borrow, not to lend.
reason.id: Ini adalah kebalikan dari permintaan wanita tersebut; dialah yang meminta untuk meminjam, bukan untuk meminjamkan.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: You said that you wanted to go shopping this afternoon. What do you want to get?
W: I think I'd like to get my dad a new wallet for his birthday.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman mentions the wallet is for her dad's birthday, not her own.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita tersebut menyebutkan dompet itu untuk ulang tahun ayahnya, bukan ulang tahunnya sendiri.

options.text: She's looking for a birthday gift.

reason.en: This is correct. The woman states she wants to get a wallet for her dad's birthday, which is a synonym for looking for a birthday gift.
reason.id: Ini benar. Wanita tersebut menyatakan ingin membelikan dompet untuk ulang tahun ayahnya, yang merupakan sinonim dari mencari hadiah ulang tahun.

options.text: She wants to go shopping with herdad.

reason.en: This is incorrect because she is going shopping to buy a gift for her dad, not to go shopping with him.
reason.id: Ini salah karena dia pergi berbelanja untuk membelikan hadiah bagi ayahnya, bukan untuk pergi berbelanja bersamanya.

options.text: She wants a new wallet for herself.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the woman explicitly states the wallet is for her dad, not for herself.
reason.id: Ini salah karena wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan dompet itu untuk ayahnya, bukan untuk dirinya sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: You didn't go into the pool, even for a quick dip?
M: I put my big toe in and decided that the water was too cold for me.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: This contradicts the man's statement that the water was too cold for him.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria tersebut bahwa airnya terlalu dingin baginya.

reason.en: This is a literal interpretation of the idiom 'big toe' and is a distractor based on surface-level meaning.
reason.id: Ini adalah interpretasi harfiah dari idiom 'big toe' dan merupakan pengecoh berdasarkan makna permukaan.

reason.en: This is incorrect because the man explicitly stated the water was too cold, not refreshing.
reason.id: Ini salah karena pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan airnya terlalu dingin, bukan menyegarkan.

options.text: He didn't go for a swim.

reason.en: The man's last line indicates he only put his toe in and decided against swimming because of the temperature, which paraphrases to 'he didn't go for a swim'.
reason.id: Baris terakhir pria tersebut menunjukkan bahwa dia hanya memasukkan jarinya dan memutuskan untuk tidak berenang karena suhu air, yang diparafrasekan menjadi 'dia tidak jadi berenang'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line

questions.passage:
M: Do you know where your sweater is?
W think I left it at my sister's house, but I'm not sure.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She just left her sister's house.

reason.en: This option incorrectly interprets the phrase 'left it at my sister's house' as the act of leaving the house itself, rather than the sweater.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah mengartikan frasa 'left it at my sister's house' sebagai tindakan meninggalkan rumah itu sendiri, bukan sweater-nya.

options.text: Her sister is not at home.

reason.en: This is a distractor that uses the word 'sister' and 'home' but does not address the main point of the dialogue regarding the location of the sweater.
reason.id: Ini adalah pengecoh yang menggunakan kata 'sister' dan 'home' tetapi tidak membahas poin utama dialog mengenai lokasi sweater.

options.text: She's not exactly sure where her sweater is.

reason.en: This is the correct answer because it paraphrases the woman's statement 'I'm not sure' and her uncertainty about the sweater's location using synonyms.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban yang benar karena memparafrase pernyataan wanita tersebut 'I'm not sure' dan ketidakpastiannya mengenai lokasi sweater menggunakan sinonim.

options.text: She doesn't know where her sister lives.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the woman mentions her sister's house, implying she knows where it is, but she is unsure about the sweater's location.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena wanita tersebut menyebutkan rumah saudarinya, yang menyiratkan dia tahu di mana lokasinya, namun dia tidak yakin dengan lokasi sweater-nya.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
M: I need for you to work on these new accounting reports.
W: But I scarcely have time to finish the ones I already have.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY?

options.text: She doesn't have time to complete additional reports.

reason.en: The woman uses the word 'scarcely,' which is an 'almost negative' expression meaning 'hardly' or 'barely.' By saying she barely has time to finish her current work, she implies she cannot take on more.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menggunakan kata 'scarcely,' yang merupakan ekspresi 'hampir negatif' yang berarti 'hampir tidak.' Dengan mengatakan dia hampir tidak punya waktu untuk menyelesaikan pekerjaannya saat ini, dia menyiratkan bahwa dia tidak bisa menerima tugas tambahan.

options.text: She cannot finish the reports that she is already working on.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the woman says she has 'scarcely' enough time, which implies she is struggling to finish, but it does not mean she is incapable of finishing them entirely.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena wanita tersebut mengatakan dia 'hampir tidak' punya cukup waktu, yang menyiratkan dia sedang berjuang untuk menyelesaikannya, namun bukan berarti dia tidak mampu menyelesaikannya sama sekali.

options.text: She is scared of having responsibility for the reports.

reason.en: This is a trap based on the similar-sounding word 'scarcely' and 'scared.' There is no evidence in the dialogue that she is afraid of the responsibility.
reason.id: Ini adalah jebakan berdasarkan kata yang terdengar mirip antara 'scarcely' dan 'scared.' Tidak ada bukti dalam dialog bahwa dia takut akan tanggung jawab tersebut.

options.text: It is not time for the accounting reports to be compiled.

reason.en: This option misinterprets the woman's statement about her lack of time as a statement about the schedule or deadline of the reports themselves.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah menafsirkan pernyataan wanita tersebut mengenai kurangnya waktu sebagai pernyataan mengenai jadwal atau tenggat waktu laporan itu sendiri.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 9: Listen for 'Almost Negative' Expressions

questions.passage:
W: How much longer do you think you're going to stay on that exercise machine?
M: I give up!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

reason.en: The phrase 'I give up' is an idiom meaning to stop doing something or to quit. In this context, it directly answers the woman's question about how much longer he will stay on the machine by indicating he is finished.
reason.id: Frasa 'I give up' adalah idiom yang berarti berhenti melakukan sesuatu atau menyerah. Dalam konteks ini, frasa tersebut menjawab langsung pertanyaan wanita tentang berapa lama lagi dia akan berada di mesin latihan dengan mengindikasikan bahwa dia sudah selesai.

options.text: He's going to give himself a reward for the hard work.

reason.en: This is an incorrect interpretation of the idiom 'give up'. It confuses the literal meaning of 'give' with the idiomatic meaning of quitting.
reason.id: Ini adalah interpretasi yang salah dari idiom 'give up'. Pilihan ini mencampuradukkan arti harfiah dari 'give' dengan arti idiomatik dari berhenti/menyerah.

options.text: He's going to stay on for quite some time.

reason.en: This contradicts the meaning of the idiom 'I give up', which signals the end of an activity rather than its continuation.
reason.id: Ini bertentangan dengan arti dari idiom 'I give up', yang menandakan berakhirnya suatu aktivitas, bukan kelanjutannya.

options.text: He would like to give the woman an exercise machine as a gift.

reason.en: This is a trap answer that uses the word 'give' from the dialogue to create a false connection, ignoring the idiomatic meaning of the phrase.
reason.id: Ini adalah jawaban jebakan yang menggunakan kata 'give' dari dialog untuk menciptakan hubungan palsu, dengan mengabaikan arti idiomatik dari frasa tersebut.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 17: Listen for Idioms

questions.passage:
W: Look at those waves coming in. They're as huge as I've ever seen them.
M: You can say that again!
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He cannot see the huge waves.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the man clearly acknowledges the size of the waves, implying he can see them.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena pria tersebut dengan jelas mengakui ukuran ombak, yang menyiratkan bahwa dia dapat melihatnya.

options.text: The waves are not coming in.

reason.en: This option contradicts the dialogue, as the man's response confirms the woman's observation about the waves.
reason.id: Opsi ini bertentangan dengan dialog, karena respons pria tersebut mengonfirmasi pengamatan wanita itu tentang ombak.

options.text: He would like the woman to repeat what she said.

reason.en: This is a literal interpretation trap. 'You can say that again' is an idiom for agreement, not a request for repetition.
reason.id: Ini adalah jebakan interpretasi harfiah. 'You can say that again' adalah idiom untuk menyatakan persetujuan, bukan permintaan untuk mengulangi perkataan.

reason.en: The expression 'You can say that again' is a common idiom used to show strong agreement with the previous speaker.
reason.id: Ekspresi 'You can say that again' adalah idiom umum yang digunakan untuk menunjukkan persetujuan kuat dengan pembicara sebelumnya.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 11: Listen for Expressions of Agreement

questions.passage:
M: Are you ready for the political science exam today? I stayed up all night studying for it.
W: Didn't you know that the professor put it off until next week?
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

reason.en: The woman uses the phrasal verb 'put off,' which means to postpone or delay. Therefore, the statement that the exam was postponed is the correct paraphrase.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menggunakan phrasal verb 'put off,' yang berarti menunda. Oleh karena itu, pernyataan bahwa ujian tersebut ditunda adalah parafrasa yang tepat.

options.text: The man should have studied harder.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the woman's statement focuses on the rescheduling of the exam, not on the man's study habits.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena pernyataan wanita tersebut berfokus pada penjadwalan ulang ujian, bukan pada kebiasaan belajar pria tersebut.

options.text: Night is the best time to study for exams.

reason.en: This is a distractor that mentions 'night' and 'study' to lure the listener, but it does not address the meaning of the woman's response.
reason.id: Ini adalah pengecoh yang menyebutkan 'malam' dan 'belajar' untuk memancing pendengar, namun tidak menjawab makna dari respons wanita tersebut.

options.text: She is completely prepared for the exam.

reason.en: This option is incorrect because the woman does not mention her own preparation; she is informing the man that the exam date has changed.
reason.id: Opsi ini salah karena wanita tersebut tidak menyebutkan persiapannya sendiri; dia hanya menginformasikan kepada pria tersebut bahwa tanggal ujian telah berubah.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE - LISTENING - Skill 16: Listen for Two- and Three-Part Verbs

questions.passage:
W: I haven't turned in my schedule change form yet. Do you think that's a problem?
M: You haven't turned it in yet? It's absolutely essential that you turn the form in immediately.
questions.question: WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Students who want to change schedules should form a line.

reason.en: This option is a distractor that uses the word 'form' from the dialogue to create a false meaning related to waiting in a line, which is not mentioned.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah pengecoh yang menggunakan kata 'form' dari dialog untuk menciptakan makna palsu yang berkaitan dengan mengantre, yang tidak disebutkan sama sekali.

options.text: It is only possible to make four changes in the schedule.

reason.en: This option is a distractor that uses the word 'four' (similar sound to 'form') to confuse the listener, which is irrelevant to the context.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah pengecoh yang menggunakan kata 'four' (suara mirip dengan 'form') untuk membingungkan pendengar, yang tidak relevan dengan konteks.

options.text: It is necessary to submit the form quickly.

reason.en: The man emphasizes that it is 'absolutely essential' to turn the form in 'immediately,' which is a synonym for submitting it quickly.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menekankan bahwa sangat penting untuk menyerahkan formulir tersebut 'segera' (immediately), yang merupakan sinonim dari menyerahkannya dengan cepat.

options.text: Problems occur when people don't wait their turn.

reason.en: This option is a distractor that misinterprets the word 'problem' mentioned in the woman's question, creating a scenario not supported by the dialogue.
reason.id: Opsi ini adalah pengecoh yang menyalahartikan kata 'problem' yang disebutkan dalam pertanyaan wanita tersebut, menciptakan skenario yang tidak didukung oleh dialog.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 1: Focus on the Last Line and Skill 2: Choose Answers with Synonyms

questions.passage:
W: I'd like to try on some rings, please.
M: Do you prefer rings in gold or silver?

questions.tested_skill: SECTION ONE — LISTENING - Skill 17: Listen for Idioms

parts.title: Part B
parts.instruction:
Directions: In this part of the test, you will hear longer conversations. After each conversation, you 
will hear several questions. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. 
After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the best 
answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 
Remember, you are not allowed to take notes or write in your test book.

questions.question: 31. WHAT SEEMS TO BE TRUE ABOUT BOB'S HAIRCUT?

reason.en: The man explicitly states, 'It's a lot shorter,' and expresses shock at his appearance, confirming the haircut is unusually short.
reason.id: Pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan, 'It's a lot shorter,' dan mengungkapkan keterkejutan atas penampilannya, yang mengonfirmasi bahwa potongan rambutnya sangat pendek.

reason.en: There is no information in the conversation suggesting this is Bob's first haircut; the woman mentions it is shorter than 'usual'.
reason.id: Tidak ada informasi dalam percakapan yang menunjukkan bahwa ini adalah potongan rambut pertama Bob; wanita tersebut menyebutkan bahwa rambutnya lebih pendek dari 'biasanya'.

options.text: Bob doesn't know who gave him the haircut

reason.en: The man says he doesn't recognize himself in the mirror, not that he is unaware of who performed the haircut.
reason.id: Pria tersebut mengatakan dia tidak mengenali dirinya sendiri di cermin, bukan berarti dia tidak tahu siapa yang memotong rambutnya.

options.text: After the haircut, Bob's hair still touches the floor.

reason.en: The phrase 'went to town' is an idiom meaning to do something with great intensity or excess, not that the hair physically touches the floor.
reason.id: Frasa 'went to town' adalah idiom yang berarti melakukan sesuatu dengan intensitas atau berlebihan, bukan berarti rambutnya secara fisik menyentuh lantai.

Crocodiles always appear to have tears in their eyes, but they are not crying because of sadness, or even pretended sadness. Instead, a crocodile uses its tear ducts to get rid of extra salt from its body. A crocodile 
does not sweat the same way that humans do and must get rid of extra salt through tears. So if you see a crying crocodile, do not think that it's feeling sad; it is basically sweating through its eyes. 

Look! Over there on the right. There are two large crocodiles on the water's edge, right next to the fallen trees. You can get out your cameras and take pictures from here on the tram, but no, you cannot get off the tram to get any closer.
groups.label: Q39-42

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to the following lecture by a 
university professor. 
(woman) Please take your seats now because I would like to begin today's lecture. Today, we will be discussing one of the more elegant and distinct forms of nineteenth-century transportation—the clipper ship. 

Clipper ships of the nineteenth century were the graceful, multisailed, oceangoing vessels that were designed for maximum speed. They were given the name "clipper" ship in reference to the fact that they "clipped along" at such a fast rate of speed. 

Clipper ships were constructed with a large number of sails in order to maximize their speed. They often had six to eight sails on each of the masts, and ships commonly had three and 
perhaps four masts. The speeds that they achieved were unbelievably fast for the era; clipper ships could, for example, accomplish the amazing feat of traveling from New York to San Francisco in less than a hundred days.

Clipper ships first came into use in the United States in the 1840s. They were originally intended to make the trip from New York, around the tip of South America, and on to China in order to transport tea to the United States. Once gold was discovered in California in 1848, clipper ships were immediately put into use to carry large numbers of gold prospectors and large amounts of mining supplies from the East Coast to California. With the success of the American clipper ships, the British began their own fleet of clipper ships to transport goods from the far reaches of the 
British Empire.
groups.label: Q43-46

groups.passage:
(narrator) Listen to the following talk by a drama coach to a group of actors. 

(man) I know that some of you are feeling more than a little nervous about tonight's performance, and I want you to understand that this is quite a natural feeling. You are going to be on a stage in front of a lot of people tonight, and it's normal to be experiencing some nerves. I would like to help you to understand these feelings and not to let them interfere with your performance. 

What you are experiencing is called stage fright. Stage fright is the fear that develops before you give a performance in front of an audience. Stage fright is not just experienced by actors and actresses; it can also be experienced by musicians, athletes, teachers—anyone who performs in front of a group of people. It occurs before a performance when a performer is concerned about looking foolish in front of others. Just before tonight's performance, if you are feeling a bit tense, if your knees are shaking, if your stomach has butterflies in it, and if you are thinking about how bad your performance could be, then you have a major case of stage fright. 

To control stage fright, you can work to control both the physical reactions and the negative thoughts. To combat the physical reactions, you can try techniques such as deep breathing, muscle relaxation, or even just laughing to relieve some of the pressure. To combat the negative thoughts, you should force yourself to focus on what you have to do rather than on what other people are going to think. 

That's all I have to say for now. I'll see you back here at six o'clock because the performance starts at eight o'clock. Just remember that if you begin to feel at all nervous, try some deep breathing to relax and focus your thoughts on the performance that you are about to give. See you his evening.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO - STRUCTURE & WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 11: Use Adjective Clause Connectors Correctly

parts.title: Written Expression
parts.instruction: In questions 16-40, each sentence has four underlined words or phrases. The four underlined parts of the sentence are marked (A), (B), (C), and (D). Identify the one underlined word or phrase that must be changed in order for the sentence to be correct. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen.

questions.question: Q: Mosquitoes will ~[accepts]~(A) the ~[malaria]~(B) parasite at ~[only one]~(C) stage of the parasite's ~[complex]~(D) life cycle.

reason.en: The word 'accepts' is incorrect because it follows the modal auxiliary 'will'. According to Skill 32, modals must be followed by the base form of the verb. The correct form is 'accept'.
reason.id: Kata 'accepts' salah karena mengikuti kata kerja bantu modal 'will'. Berdasarkan Skill 32, modal harus diikuti oleh bentuk dasar (base form) dari kata kerja. Bentuk yang benar adalah 'accept'.

reason.en: 'Malaria' is a noun used correctly here as an attributive noun modifying 'parasite'.
reason.id: 'Malaria' adalah kata benda yang digunakan dengan benar di sini sebagai kata benda atributif yang memodifikasi 'parasite'.

reason.en: 'Only one' is a correct determiner phrase used to specify the quantity of the stage in the life cycle.
reason.id: 'Only one' adalah frasa penentu yang benar yang digunakan untuk menentukan jumlah tahapan dalam siklus hidup.

reason.en: 'Complex' is an adjective correctly modifying the noun phrase 'life cycle'.
reason.id: 'Complex' adalah kata sifat yang memodifikasi frasa kata benda 'life cycle' dengan benar.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 32: Use of the Verb (Modal + Base Form)

questions.question: Q: The ~[counterpart]~(A) ~[of]~(B) a negative ~[electrons]~(C) is the ~[positive]~(D) proton.

reason.en: The word 'counterpart' is a singular noun used correctly here to refer to the corresponding entity of an electron.
reason.id: Kata 'counterpart' adalah kata benda tunggal yang digunakan dengan tepat di sini untuk merujuk pada entitas yang bersesuaian dengan elektron.

reason.en: The preposition 'of' is correctly used to show the relationship between the noun 'counterpart' and the following noun phrase.
reason.id: Preposisi 'of' digunakan dengan tepat untuk menunjukkan hubungan antara kata benda 'counterpart' dan frasa kata benda berikutnya.

reason.en: This is the error. The adjective 'negative' modifies a singular noun. Since the proton is singular, 'electrons' should be the singular 'electron'.
reason.id: Ini adalah kesalahannya. Kata sifat 'negative' memodifikasi kata benda tunggal. Karena proton bersifat tunggal, 'electrons' seharusnya berbentuk tunggal yaitu 'electron'.

reason.en: The adjective 'positive' is correctly used to describe the noun 'proton'.
reason.id: Kata sifat 'positive' digunakan dengan tepat untuk mendeskripsikan kata benda 'proton'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 39: Use the Correct Singular or Plural Noun

questions.question: Q: The ankle joint ~[occur]~(A) where the ~[lower]~(B) ends of the tibia and fibula slot ~[neatly]~(C) ~[around]~(D) the talus.

It should be the plural form 'occur' if the subject were plural, but in this context, the verb should be 'occurs' to agree with the singular concept of the joint, or more accurately, the verb 'occurs' is needed to match the singular subject 'The ankle joint'.
reason.id: Kata kerja 'occur' salah karena tidak sesuai dengan subjek jamak 'the lower ends of the tibia and fibula'. Seharusnya menggunakan bentuk 'occurs' agar sesuai dengan subjek tunggal 'The ankle joint'.

reason.en: The word 'lower' is used correctly as an adjective modifying the noun 'ends'.
reason.id: Kata 'lower' digunakan dengan benar sebagai kata sifat yang menerangkan kata benda 'ends'.

reason.en: The word 'neatly' is an adverb correctly modifying the verb 'slot', describing how the bones fit together.
reason.id: Kata 'neatly' adalah kata keterangan yang benar untuk menerangkan kata kerja 'slot', menjelaskan bagaimana tulang-tulang tersebut terpasang.

reason.en: The preposition 'around' is used correctly to describe the spatial relationship between the bones and the talus.
reason.id: Kata depan 'around' digunakan dengan benar untuk mendeskripsikan hubungan spasial antara tulang-tulang tersebut dengan talus.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 1: Be Sure the Sentence Has a Subject and a Verb

questions.question: Q: In the United States and Canada, ~[motor vehicle]~(A) laws ~[affect]~(B) the ~[operate]~(C) of motorcycles ~[as well as]~(D) automobiles.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 39: Nouns (Use the correct form)

questions.question: Q: The neocortex ~[is]~(A), in evolutionary ~[terms]~(B), ~[most]~(C) recent ~[layer]~(D) of the brain.

reason.id: Ini adalah kesalahannya. Kalimat ini membandingkan neocortex dengan lapisan otak lainnya. Karena membandingkan neocortex dengan seluruh bagian otak (bukan hanya dua hal), maka bentuk superlatif 'the most' diperlukan, dan harus didahului oleh artikel 'the'.

reason.en: The word 'layer' is a singular countable noun used correctly after the superlative adjective to identify the neocortex.
reason.id: Kata 'layer' adalah kata benda tunggal yang dapat dihitung dan digunakan dengan benar setelah kata sifat superlatif untuk mengidentifikasi neocortex.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 27: Comparatives and Superlatives

questions.question: Q: There ~[are]~(A) more than eighty-four million ~[specimens]~(B) in the National Museum of Natural History's ~[collection]~(C) of biological, geological, archeological, and ~[anthropology]~(D) treasures.

The sentence requires parallel structure for the list of adjectives modifying 'treasures': biological (adjective), geological (adjective), archeological (adjective), and anthropological (adjective). 'Anthropology' is a noun and must be changed to the adjective 'anthropological'.

Kalimat ini memerlukan struktur paralel untuk daftar kata sifat yang memodifikasi 'treasures': biological (kata sifat), geological (kata sifat), archeological (kata sifat), dan anthropological (kata sifat). 'Anthropology' adalah kata benda dan harus diubah menjadi kata sifat 'anthropological'.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 25: Parallel Structure

questions.question: Q: ~[After]~(A) George Washington married ~[widow]~(B) Martha Custis, the couple ~[came]~(C) to ~[resides]~(D) at Mount Vernon.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 32: Use of the Verb (Base Form after Modals/Infinitives)

questions.question: Q: At this stage in ~[their]~(A) development, ~[rubberized]~(B) asphalt can ~[hardly]~(C) be classified ~[as]~(D) cutting edge.

reason.id: 'Hardly' adalah kata keterangan derajat yang digunakan dengan benar di sini untuk menerangkan frasa kata kerja pasif 'be classified'.

questions.question: Q: ~[A]~(A) zoom lens ~[produces]~(B) an ~[inverted]~(C) real image, either on the film in a camera ~[and]~(D) on the light-sensitive tube of a television camera.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO - WRITTEN EXPRESSION - Skill 39: Use the Correct Singular or Plural Noun

questions.tested_skill: SECTION 2 - Skill 20: Subject/Verb Agreement

questions.question: Q: The newsreels of Hearst Metronome News, which formed part of ~[every]~(A) moviegoer's experience in the era ~[before television]~(B), offer ~[an]~(C) unique record of the ~[events]~(D) of the 1930s.

questions.tested_skill: SECTION TWO - Skill 52: Use Articles Correctly (A/An)

sections.instruction:
Time—25 minutes 
(including the reading of the directions) 
Now set your clock for 25 minutes. 
This section is designed to measure your ability to recognize language that is appropriate for standard written English. There are two types of questions in this section, with special directions for each type.

sections.title: SECTION 3  READING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part Questions 1-10
parts.instruction:
In this section you will read several passages. Each one is followed by a number of questions about it. You are to choose the one best answer, (A), (B), (C), or (D), to each question. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen. 
Answer all questions about the information in a passage on the basis of what is stated or implied in 
that passage.

questions.passage:
The hippopotamus is the third largest land animal, smaller only than the elephant and the rhinoceros. Its name comes from two Greek words which mean "river horse." The long name of this animal is often shortened to the easier to handle term "hippo." 

The hippo has a natural affinity for the water. It does not float on top of the water; instead, it can easily walk along the bottom of a body of water. The hippo commonly remains underwater for three to five minutes and has been known to stay under for up to half an hour before coming up for air. 

In spite of its name, the hippo has relatively little in common with the horse and instead has a number of interesting similarities in common with the whale. When a hippo comes up after a stay at the bottom of a lake or river, it releases air through a blowhole, just like a whale. In addition, the hippo resembles the whale in that they both have thick layers of blubber for protection and they are almost completely hairless.

John James Audubon, nineteenth-century artist and naturalist, is known as one of the foremost authorities on North American birds. Born in Les Cayes, Haiti, in 1785, Audubon was raised in France and studied art under French artist Jacques-Louis David. After settling on his father's  Pennsylvania estate at the age of eighteen, he first began to study and paint birds. 

In his young adulthood, Audubon undertook numerous enterprises, generally without a tremendous amount of success; at various times during his life he was involved in a mercantile business, a lumber and grist mill, a taxidermy business, and a school. His general mode of operating a business was to leave it either unattended or in the hands of a partner and take off on excursions through the wilds to paint the natural life that he saw. His business career came to end in 1819 when he was jailed for debt and forced to file for bankruptcy. 

It was at that time that Audubon began seriously to pursue the dream of publishing a collection of his paintings of birds. For the next six years he painted birds in their natural habitats while his wife worked as a teacher to support the family. His Birds of America, which included engravings of 435 of his colorful and lifelike water colors, was published in parts during the period from 1826 to 1838 in England. After the success of the English editions, American editions of his work were published in 1839, and his fame and fortune were ensured.

Born in Les Cayes, Haiti, in 1785, Audubon was raised in France and studied art under French artist Jacques-Louis David. After settling on his father's  Pennsylvania estate at the age of eighteen, he first began to study and paint birds. 

In his young adulthood, Audubon undertook numerous enterprises, generally without a tremendous amount of success; at various times during his life he was involved in a mercantile business, a lumber and grist mill, a taxidermy business, and a school. His general mode of operating a business was to leave it either unattended or in the hands of a partner and take off on excursions through the wilds to paint the natural life that he saw. His business career came to end in 1819 when he was jailed for debt and forced to file for bankruptcy. 

It was at that time that Audubon began seriously to pursue the dream of publishing a collection of his paintings of birds. For the next six years he painted birds in their natural habitats while his wife worked as a teacher to support the family. His Birds of America, which included engravings of 435 of his colorful and lifelike water colors, was published in parts during the period from 1826 to 1838 in England. After the success of the English editions, American editions of his work were published in 1839, and his fame and fortune were ensured.

questions.passage:
Schizophrenia is often confused with multiple personality disorder yet is quite distinct from it. Schizophrenia is one of the more common mental disorders, considerably more common than multiple personality disorder. The term "schizophrenia" is composed of roots which mean "a splitting of the mind," but it does not refer to a division into separate and distinct personalities, as occurs in multiple personality disorder. Instead, schizophrenic behavior is generally characterized by illogical thought patterns and withdrawal from reality. Schizophrenics often live in a fantasy world where they hear voices that others cannot hear, often voices of famous people. Schizophrenics tend to withdraw from families and friends and communicate mainly with the "voices" that they hear in their minds. 

It is common for the symptoms of schizophrenia to develop during the late teen years or early twenties, but the causes of schizophrenia are not well understood. It is believed that heredity may play a part in the onset of schizophrenia. In addition, abnormal brain chemistry also seems to have a role; certain brain chemicals, called neurotransmitters, have been found to be at abnormal levels in some schizophrenics.

Giannini established this bank on October 17, 1904, in a renovated saloon in San Francisco's Italian community of North Beach under the name Bank of Italy, with immigrants and first-time bank customers comprising the majority of his first customers. During its development, Giannini s bank survived major crises in the form of a natural disaster and a major economic upheaval that not all other banks were able to overcome. 

One major test for Gianninis bank occurred on April 18, 1906, when a massive earthquake struck San Francisco, followed by a raging fire that destroyed much of the city. Giannini obtained two wagons and teams of horses, filled the wagons with the bank's reserves, mostly in the form of gold, (JO) covered the reserves with crates of oranges, and escaped from the chaos of the city with his clients' funds protected. questions.passage:
The organization that today is known as the Bank of America did start out in America, but under quite a different name. Italian American A.P. Giannini established this bank on October 17, 1904, in a renovated saloon in San Francisco's Italian community of North Beach under the name Bank of Italy, with immigrants and first-time bank customers comprising the majority of his first customers. During its development, Giannini s bank survived major crises in the form of a natural disaster and a major economic upheaval that not all other banks were able to overcome. 

One major test for Gianninis bank occurred on April 18, 1906, when a massive earthquake struck San Francisco, followed by a raging fire that destroyed much of the city. Giannini obtained two wagons and teams of horses, filled the wagons with the bank's reserves, mostly in the form of gold, (JO) covered the reserves with crates of oranges, and escaped from the chaos of the city with his clients' funds protected. In the aftermath of the disaster, Gianninis bank was the first to resume operations. Unable to install the bank in a proper office setting, Giannini opened up shop on the Washington Street Wharf on a makeshift desk created from boards and barrels. 

In the period following the 1906 fire, the Bank of Italy continued to prosper and expand. By 1918 there were twenty-four branches of the Bank of Italy, and by 1928 Giannini had acquired numerous other banks, including a Bank of America located in New York City. In 1930 he consolidated all the branches of the Bank of Italy, the Bank of America in New York City, and another Bank of America that he had formed in California into the Bank of America National Trust and Savings Association. 

A second major crisis for the bank occurred during the Great Depression of the 1930s. Although Giannini had already retired prior to the darkest days of the Depression, he Became incensed when his successor began selling off banks during the bad economic times. Giannini resumed leadership of the bank at the age of sixty-two. Under Gianninis leadership, the bank weathered the storm of the Depression and subsequently moved into a phase of overseas development.

sections.instruction:
Time—55 minutes 
(including the reading of the directions) 
Now set your clock for 55 minutes. 

This section is designed to measure your ability to read and understand short passages similar in topic and style to those that students are likely to encounter in North American universities and colleges. 

Directions; In this section you will read several passages. Each one is followed by a number of questions about it. You are to choose the one best answer, (A), (B), (C), or (D), to each question. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that corresponds to the 
letter of the answer you have chosen. 

Answer all questions about the information in a passage on the basis of what is stated or implied in that passage.
